question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHookStorage(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the createdTimestamp field exposed in HookStorage responses to determine when a staking reward hook was first created, correlating this with on-chain events to identify the reward accrual start date and calculate the total unpaid reward amount?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHookStorageChange(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 0 as created_timestamp aliasing in the historical query (which always returns 0 for created_timestamp) to confuse downstream consumers about the age of staking reward entries, potentially causing incorrect reward calculations in systems that rely on this field?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHookStorage(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the modifiedTimestamp field in HookStorage to determine the last time a staking reward storage slot was updated, identifying accounts with stale (long-unclaimed) rewards that represent high-value theft targets?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHooks(HooksRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the PageRequest.of(0, request.getLimit(), sort) pagination (always starting at page 0) combined with cursor-based navigation to enumerate all hooks for a victim account across multiple requests, with no server-side session state preventing repeated enumeration?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHookStorageChange(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findByKeyInAndTimestampBetween uses key in (:keys) with a Collection parameter to inject a large number of keys in a single query, causing a database performance degradation (DoS) that delays legitimate staking reward claims while the attacker monitors the reward state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHookStorage(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the HookStorage.Id composite key (hookId, key, ownerId) to craft queries that enumerate all storage entries across different hookIds for the same ownerId, systematically mapping the entire staking reward storage layout of a victim account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHookStorageChange(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of pagination enforcement on the historical query path to retrieve an unbounded number of staking reward storage change records, overwhelming the response and extracting the complete reward history in a single request?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHooks(HooksRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Sort.Direction parameter (ASC/DESC) to control the ordering of returned hooks, using DESC to find the highest-numbered (most recently created) staking reward hooks that are most likely to hold large unclaimed rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHookStorage(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DomainUtils.leftPadBytes(key, KEY_BYTE_LENGTH) normalization in HookStorage.setKey() to craft key queries that match storage slots after left-padding, bypassing intended key isolation and reading staking reward values stored under padded key representations?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHookStorageChange(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the value_written is null or length(value_written) = 0 as deleted logic in the historical query to identify storage slots that were explicitly zeroed out (reward claimed/reset), then use the timestamp to determine the exact moment a reward was claimed and correlate with network transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHookStorage(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getKeysInRange() uses Arrays.compareUnsigned for byte array comparison while the database may use a different collation for the key column, causing a mismatch where keys filtered out client-side are still returned by the database query, leaking additional staking reward storage entries?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHooks(HooksRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the entityService.lookup() call to perform account alias resolution, supplying an EVM address alias for a victim account to discover their canonical entity ID and then enumerate all their staking reward hooks without knowing the numeric account ID?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHookStorageChange(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the timestamp.getAdjustedLowerRangeValue() and timestamp.adjustUpperBound() methods to supply boundary timestamps that span staking reward period boundaries, extracting reward accrual data across multiple staking periods and calculating the total unpaid reward for a victim account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHookStorage(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the HookStorageResult record exposing both ownerId and storage collection to correlate the resolved canonical entity ID with specific storage values, enabling precise identification of staking reward amounts tied to a specific account even when querying by alias?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHookStorageChange(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a check that keysInRange is non-empty before calling findByKeyBetweenAndTimestampBetween (only checked when requestHasKeys is true) to perform an unrestricted full-table scan of hook_storage_change for a victim's hook, extracting all staking reward write history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHooks(HooksRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hookIds.stream().filter() range intersection logic to perform a timing side-channel attack, measuring response time differences between requests that hit the database versus those that return early (idsInRange.isEmpty()), inferring the existence of specific hook IDs for a victim account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHookStorage(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the isHistorical() check (which only tests if timestamp Bound is non-empty) to switch between current and historical query paths by simply adding or omitting a timestamp parameter, bypassing the deleted=false filter on the current path and accessing deleted staking reward entries via the historical path?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHookStorageChange(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the consensus_timestamp as consensus_timestamp aliasing in the historical query result to obtain the exact network consensus timestamp of each staking reward write, enabling precise correlation with block explorers to identify the triggering transaction and front-run future reward claims?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHookStorage(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DomainUtils.trim(value) normalization applied to HookStorage values to infer the actual byte length of staking reward values from the trimmed response, deducing the magnitude of unpaid rewards even when the raw value is not directly interpretable?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHooks(HooksRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween query with lowerBound=0 and upperBound=Long.MAX_VALUE to retrieve all hooks for any account in a single paginated sweep, identifying staking reward hooks by their hook type or metadata without any access control preventing cross-account enumeration?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHookStorageChange(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the historical query returns value_written (not value_read) as the value field, allowing them to observe the exact byte sequence written to staking reward storage slots and decode the reward amount using knowledge of the hook's storage encoding scheme?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHookStorage(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the PageRequest sort by Constants.KEY in ASC order to retrieve staking reward storage entries in deterministic key order, enabling systematic binary search over the key space to locate specific reward balance slots with minimal requests?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHookStorageChange(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the dual sort in getPageRequest() for historical queries (Sort.Order(order, KEY), Sort.Order(DESC, CONSENSUS_TIMESTAMP)) to retrieve the most recent write to each staking reward storage slot, effectively reading the current reward state via the historical path even when the current-state path is unavailable?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHooks(HooksRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any check that the ownerId in the request corresponds to the authenticated caller to read hooks belonging to any account on the network, since the service layer performs no session-to-entity binding before calling hookRepository.findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getHookStorage(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the HookStorage composite ID (ownerId, hookId, key) to craft a targeted query for a specific known storage slot (e.g., slot 0 which often holds the primary balance in EVM contracts) to directly read the unpaid staking reward balance of any account?
